summary:
Alkaline phosphatase promotes calcification by producing inorganic phosphate essential for hydroxyapatite formation, while pyrophosphatase inhibits excessive or pathologic calcification by decomposing pyrophosphate. Together, these enzymes maintain balanced mineralization through opposing yet coordinated actions.  

\boxed{C}
